Notice Of Motion And Motion To Schedule Default Judgment Oral Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Oct-27-2015 Off Calendar

AMENDED MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-21-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ROBERT N. WEAVER, ESQ. of Less & Weaver, Tel No. 415-398-9800 appearing for Plaintiff Less & Weaver;
Atty. NOAH S. ROSENTHAL, ESQ. of Long & Levit LLP, Tel No. 415-397-2222 appearing for Defendant H. Kenneth Ahn.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT H. KENNETH AHN'S AMENDED MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND, the motion for relief from default judgment is denied. The facts presented on this motion do not support Mr. Anh's position that he is entitled to relief on the grounds of surprise or excusable neglect. Mr. Anh's assertion that he was in the middle of settlement negotiations and this led him to believe that he had additional time to respond to Plaintiff's complaint is refuted by his own admission in the April 21, 2015, email to Mr. Weaver that his answer was due in a "few days." Mr. Anh's contention that he did not need to respond because he was in the midst of a settlement negotiation with Plaintiff is also refuted by the fact that an extension to the answer had not been granted and Mr. Anh waited ten weeks after the April 29, 2015, meeting to communicate with Plaintiff. Furthermore, Mr. Anh has failed to present a reasonable explanation as to why he knew that his response to Plaintiff's complaint was due within 30 days of service but that he did not know that Plaintiff would file a default within ten days of the answer due date if an answer was not provided. Finally, Mr. Anh reliance upon Smith v. Los Angeles Bookbinders Union No. 63 (1955) 133 Cal.App.2d 486, 500 to support his position that advanced notice is needed prior to filing an entry of default is misplaced as the facts of Smith are entirely distinguishable from the case at hand. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

AMENDED MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-13-2015 Continued To Oct-21-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT H. AHN'S AMENDED MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND, Judge Harold Kahn, who is handling the October 13 law and motion calendar, has recused himself from this case. The motion will be heard by another judge on October 21, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
